zh.lifeSummary: 當William Peter de Pierrepont Empson, Knight Sir於從1418年1月10日到1419年1月9日出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特時，他的父親Sir Francis Empson29歲，母親Margaret Burdon27歲。他於大約1441年與Lady Elizabeth Joseph在英國諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於從1473年1月10日到1474年1月9日在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Peter de Pierrepont Empson, Knight Sir于从1418年1月10日到1419年1月9日出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特时，他的父亲Sir Francis Empson29岁，母亲Margaret Burdon27岁。他于大约1441年与Lady Elizabeth Joseph在英国諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于从1473年1月10日到1474年1月9日在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Empson,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: metronymic meaning ‘son of Emma’, from the Middle English female personal name Em(e), Emme (see Imm ) + -son.
shortened form of Emerson . The two names were no doubt confused, and it is possible that the reverse process sometimes occurred, whereby Em(m)erson was an altered form of Emmeson, i.e. Em(p)son.

variants.name: Imeson
meanings: English: variant either of Emmeson (see Empson ) or of Emerson .

variants.name: Impson
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Empson  . This form of the surname is now more common in the US than in Britain.

variants.name: Imm
meanings: German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Im(m)o, a pet form of names based on Irmin-, a divine or ethnic name formed with irmin, ermen ‘whole, entire’.

English (Wiltshire): from the ancient Germanic female personal name Imma, Emma, of the same origin as in 1 above. In Old English Imma, Emma was borne by both males and females. In Middle English, under Norman influence, the personal name Emm(a), Imm(a) came to be used almost exclusively for women, being taken as a short form of Ermingard.

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

nameMeanings.name: Pierrepont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When William Peter de Pierrepont Empson, Knight Sir was born from 10 January 1418 to 9 January 1419, in Towcester, Northamptonshire, England, his father, Sir Francis Empson, was 29 and his mother, Margaret Burdon, was 27. He married Lady Elizabeth Joseph about 1441, in Towcester, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died from 10 January 1473 to 9 January 1474, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Peter de Pierrepont Empson, Knight Sir est né du 10 janvier 1418 au 9 janvier 1419, à Towcester, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Francis Empson, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Margaret Burdon, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Lady Elizabeth Joseph environ 1441, à Towcester, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé du 10 janvier 1473 au 9 janvier 1474, dans sa ville natale.

ko.lifeSummary: William Peter de Pierrepont Empson, Knight Sir(이)가 1418년 1월 10일부터 1419년 1월 9일까지 영국 노르섬프토실르 타우스스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Francis Empson(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Burdon(은)는 27세였다. 그는 대략 1441년 영국 노르섬프토실르 타우스스터에서 Lady Elizabeth Joseph(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1473년 1월 10일부터 1474년 1월 9일까지 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Elizabeth Joseph около 1441 г. в местности тоусестир, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер с 10 января 1473 по 9 января 1474 в его родном городе.
